expectations.text: Produces a written scope document, not just a conversational answer
expectations.evidence: Full doc with Problem/Actors/User flow/Touches & dependencies/MVP cut line/Success criteria/Open questions sections, following the skill template exactly.

expectations.text: Problem statement frames what the user is trying to do and what currently blocks them, not just a restatement of 'add bookmark feature'
expectations.evidence: "저장해둘 방법이 없어 ... 결국 다시 찾지 못하고 잊어버린다" — names the blocker (no way to save) and the underlying goal.

expectations.text: Identifies a single actor explicitly rather than inventing an unnecessary second role
expectations.evidence: States plainly: "이 기능은 단일 사용자 유형(독자/로그인 사용자)만 관련된다. 별도의 운영자/관리자 역할은 v1 범위에서 필요하지 않다."

expectations.text: User flow is written as an ordered numbered sequence of steps, not a list of capabilities
expectations.evidence: 8 numbered steps plus lettered unhappy-path branches (2a, 5a, 6a).

expectations.text: MVP cut line has at least one item placed in 'out of scope (later)' or 'explicitly not doing' rather than putting everything in v1
expectations.evidence: Out of scope: folders/tags, sort/filter, notifications, sharing. Explicitly not doing: anonymous local bookmarks, quotas, export/import.

expectations.text: Success criteria are checkable/measurable statements, not aspirational ones
expectations.evidence: 4 statements like "동일한 게시물을 두 번 북마크해도 목록에 중복으로 나타나지 않는다" — each is independently verifiable.

expectations.text: Touches & dependencies section names an entry point in the existing product without designing the UI in detail
expectations.evidence: Names entry points (post list/detail page, new '내 북마크' page) and the `bookmark` entity by name only, no schema or pixel-level UI.

expectations.text: Does not add unnecessary complexity (e.g. multi-role permissions) for what the prompt described as a single-user feature
expectations.evidence: Explicitly scopes out admin/operator role; keeps to a single actor throughout.
